title: Stop extracting data: Run serverless Python natively in BigQuery!
summary: Google Cloud announced general availability of Python User Defined Functions (UDFs) in BigQuery, allowing users to run custom Python code natively, securely, and serverlessly within SQL statements. The feature supports importing libraries and running machine learning tasks without infrastructure management.
writeup: The video covers the general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, which let custom Python code run natively inside SQL statements without extracting data to separate systems. It matters because the execution is serverless and secure, removing the need to manage infrastructure for these workloads. Key takeaways: UDFs can import libraries and support machine learning tasks directly within BigQuery queries.
